title: Fixing a Broken Sieve of Eratosthenes

statement.markdown: Solve the problem and fix the bugs.
statement.inputFormat: Input will be a single integer n, representing the size of the array.
statement.outputFormat: Output should be a boolean value indicating whether the number is prime or not.
statement.notes: Optimize the Sieve of Eratosthenes algorithm to improve performance for large inputs.

hints.text: First hint text: Review the Sieve of Eratosthenes algorithm and identify the bug.

editorial.title: Sieve of Eratosthenes
editorial.intuition: Use a boolean array prime[] where index i represents the i-th prime number. Initialize all entries as true. A value in prime[i] will finally be false if i is Not a prime, else true.
commonMistakes: Incorrect implementation of the Sieve of Eratosthenes algorithm

starterCode.cpp: class Solution { public: bool isPrime(int n) { // TODO: implement }; };
starterCode.java: public class Solution { public boolean isPrime(int n) { // TODO: implement } }
starterCode.python: class Solution: def is_prime(self, n: int) -> bool: # TODO: implement pass
starterCode.javascript: function isPrime(n) { // TODO: implement return n; }

buggyStarterCode.cpp: class Solution { public: bool isPrime(int n) { int i; for (i = 2; i <= n; i++) if (n % i == 0) return false; return true; } };
buggyStarterCode.java: public class Solution { public boolean isPrime(int n) { int i; for (i = 2; i <= n; i++) if (n % i == 0) return false; return true; } }
buggyStarterCode.python: class Solution: def is_prime(self, n: int) -> bool: int i; for i in range(2, n + 1): if n % i == 0: return False; return True
buggyStarterCode.javascript: function isPrime(n) { var i; for (i = 2; i <= n; i++) if (n % i == 0) return false; return true; }
